mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing equity investors to price in lower future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand strength, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in over 60% of periods where INDPRO shows month-over-month acceleration during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 260.0 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL stays above 260.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor strength signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth rates above 2.0 percent reflect sustained economic expansion that supports corporate profit margins, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with quarterly GDP growth rates over a 5-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that precedes broader economic slowdown, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances following two consecutive monthly INDPRO declines.
